classification_summary: A primary (today's work mostly aligned with NS goals); B (Phase B over Phase C deferral — tradeoff explicit and recorded in DEC-2026-04-29-002); C (Box-scaffolding inconsistency — tradeoff unconscious; flagged below for §11 update); F (audit questions triggered by DEC-2026-04-30-002, -003, -004 — all 10 questions answered satisfactorily).
dispatch_method: in-sandbox synthesis (Cowork agent acting as the dispatched north_star_steward inline; live HTTP path via POST /api/agents/north_star_steward/run is verifiable separately by Jake's terminal — see ATOM-0042 verification field)
input_summary: ATOM-2026-04-30-0042 smoke test. Steward invoked in audit_only mode against today's work surface (2026-04-30): 14 atoms shipped, 3 new ledgers landed (Atom + Deprecation + the steward fleet), 4 new Decisions (DEC-2026-04-30-001 through -004), 3 new rules (Claim Before Doing + Announce + P shortcut), 5 of 5 ledger stewards reaching runnable state. Goal: confirm the steward produces a coherent NS alignment audit, surfaces real findings, and flags only true findings (no false positives). Cross-validates the novel north_star_steward output template (different from temporal_continuity's state-freshness audit and open_problems' inventory audit).

alignment_snapshot.are_we_pointed_at_purpose: YES — strongly. Today's Phase B steward fleet completion is among the most NS-aligned work in the project's history. Atoms compound with ledgers (NS-01); they live in versioned files (NS-02); the unified Box pattern strengthens Box-Based Local Intelligence (NS-03); the claim protocol is safe-movement-by-design (NS-05); receipts make every steward run rebuildable (NS-07); atoms ARE the agent handoff substrate (NS-09); and the recovery checklist + single-writer protocol embody defense-as-first-class (NS-10).

recent_decisions_and_ns_alignment.title: Deprecation Ledger
recent_decisions_and_ns_alignment.ns_alignment: NS-01 (preserves memory at retirement) + NS-07 (Snapshot Protocol = rebuildable)

recent_decisions_and_ns_alignment.title: Atom Ledger
recent_decisions_and_ns_alignment.ns_alignment: NS-09 (parallel handoff substrate) + NS-05 (single-writer protocol) + NS-10 (claim-before-doing defense)

recent_decisions_and_ns_alignment.title: Canonical steward path = LEDGERS/BOXES/<name>/steward/
recent_decisions_and_ns_alignment.ns_alignment: NS-03 (Box-Based Local Intelligence — stewards live with their ledgers)

recent_decisions_and_ns_alignment.title: Server-synthesized JSON for build_*.py → AnalyticsScreen
recent_decisions_and_ns_alignment.ns_alignment: NS-02 (FileTree over RAG — markdown is canonical, server synthesizes views) + NS-06 (Source-of-Truth discipline — markdown wins over JSON sidecar)

recent_tradeoffs_and_section_9_grounding.description: Phase B steward fleet completion prioritized over Phase C runtime work; PROB-016 atomized but bedrock audits queued behind steward fleet finish
recent_tradeoffs_and_section_9_grounding.ns_weighed: NS-09 (handoff continuity) over NS-07 (rebuildability) — but the tradeoff is temporary; Phase C arrives after Phase B settles

alignment_snapshot.anti_goal_proximity_warnings: NONE. Today's work moves AWAY from §4 anti-goals on every dimension: not a generic CRM drip machine, not manipulative sales automation, not disconnected files (atoms have parent_problem_id linkage), not RAG-only memory (FileTree-orchestrated atoms in JSON+MD), UI doesn't hide truth (sitemap is canonical), no silent financial commitments (atoms are infrastructure), no creepy enrichment (no customer surfaces touched today), structure increasingly understandable (3 new rules + 4 new memories + 1 first-chain-closure precedent), no fake-useful dashboards (audits cite quoted text), and the project is gaining memory faster than it's deleting it.
alignment_snapshot.wholesome_enrichment_surface_status: N/A — today's work is all ledger/atom/steward infrastructure. No client-facing copy or automation touched. §6 tests don't apply. When customer-facing work resumes, the new north_star_steward will run audits with the 4-test protocol explicitly.
alignment_snapshot.audit_questions_relevant_to_current_stat: All 10 §10 questions answered satisfactorily for the architectural-scope decisions made today (DEC-002, -003, -004) — see §F findings below.

findings_by_classification.finding: Phase B steward fleet completion is the most NS-aligned work the project has shipped in a single day. 14 atoms across 5 stewards with full audit trail.
findings_by_classification.evidence: ATOMS.md §10.1 totals: 44/30/14; activity.jsonl 313+ lines today; receipts at LEDGERS/BOXES/temporal_continuity/receipts/ + LEDGERS/BOXES/atoms/receipts/; INDEX.md row flipped for first complete chain (temporal_continuity).
findings_by_classification.recommendation: No action needed — alignment is strong. Continue.

findings_by_classification.finding: Phase B prioritized over Phase C (Box Bus runtime + atom dispatch endpoints deferred).
findings_by_classification.ns_goals_weighed: NS-09 (handoff continuity, served by stewards now) prioritized over NS-07 (full rebuildability via Box Bus runtime, deferred).
findings_by_classification.evidence: DEC-2026-04-29-002 (Three-Phase Build Discipline) explicitly orders A → B → C. PROB-2026-04-30-006 (Box Bus runtime) is `urgency: later · blocked_by: PROB-005`.
findings_by_classification.recommendation: Update §11 Alignment Notes to record that Phase B completion is the active tradeoff this week. Review trigger: Phase B reaches 5/5 runnable AND PROB-005 closed (currently 4/5 chains open; ATOM-0043 closes the parent PROB).
findings_by_classification.section_9_rule_applied: Tradeoff is explicit, dated, and reversible. Acceptable per §9.

findings_by_classification.finding: Inconsistent unified-Box scaffolding across stewards. 3 of 5 stewards (temporal_continuity, open_problems, north_star — authored by claude_cowork session) have only the `steward/` subdirectory with 3 files (AGENTS.md, prompt.md, config.json). 2 of 5 stewards (file_directory, global_ledger — authored by parallel claude_cowork sessions) have the full 6-file unified Box pattern (box.json manifest + BOX.md orientation + steward/3 files + receipts/README.md scaffolding).
ns_goals_threatened: NS-03 (Box-Based Local Intelligence — half-formed Boxes are under-equipped to be first-class participants in the Box Bus when Phase C lands)
findings_by_classification.evidence: Disk state: ls LEDGERS/BOXES/temporal_continuity/ shows BOX.md + box.json + steward/ + receipts/ (the parallel agent retroactively added these for temporal_continuity at 19:43Z). ls LEDGERS/BOXES/open_problems/ shows ONLY steward/ subdir. ls LEDGERS/BOXES/north_star/ shows ONLY steward/ subdir. ls LEDGERS/BOXES/file_directory/ + global_ledger/ shows full 6-file pattern. Surfaced in TCL §11 carry-forward but not yet in NS §11 Alignment Notes.
findings_by_classification.recommendation: Either (a) file follow-up atoms to add box.json + BOX.md + receipts/README.md to open_problems and north_star — closes the NS-03 gap and brings them to first-class Box Bus participation readiness; or (b) accept the lighter 3-file pattern explicitly in §11 with rationale, downgrade the unified-Box pattern, and update DEC-2026-04-29-015 to reflect the lighter shape. **Option (a) is recommended** because the parallel agent's 6-file pattern is what the Box Bus runtime (Phase C, PROB-006) will subscribe over — under-equipped Boxes will need backfill anyway.
findings_by_classification.section_9_rule_applied: This tradeoff is currently unconscious — the acceptance criteria for ATOMs 0028/0032/0040 said 'agents.md + prompt.md mirroring global_ledger_steward' (which itself only had 3 files at authoring time), so the lighter pattern was strictly criteria-met. The richer pattern emerged later via ATOM-0044's example. Recommend C → B promotion: explicit Decisions Ledger entry naming the 6-file pattern as canonical going forward + follow-up atoms for the 3 lighter Boxes.

findings_by_classification.finding: Three architectural decisions landed today (DEC-2026-04-30-002, -003, -004). §10 audit questions surface for each.

audit_questions_with_answers.question: Q1: Which NS goal does this support?
audit_questions_with_answers.answer: DEC-002: NS-01 + NS-07. DEC-003: NS-05 + NS-09 + NS-10. DEC-004: NS-03. All architectural; all directly aligned.

audit_questions_with_answers.question: Q2: Does this preserve or damage continuity?
audit_questions_with_answers.answer: PRESERVES on all three. Deprecation captures retirements; atoms compound the handoff substrate; unified-Box pattern keeps stewards with their ledgers.

audit_questions_with_answers.question: Q3: Easier or harder to locate truth?
audit_questions_with_answers.answer: EASIER on all three. Deprecation has explicit retirement audit trail; atoms have parent_problem_id linkage + JSON-as-canonical race resolution; unified Box pattern colocates steward with ledger so 'where does X live' becomes obvious.

audit_questions_with_answers.question: Q4: Create local memory where needed?
audit_questions_with_answers.answer: YES — per-Box receipts/, per-atom verification, per-deprecation snapshot.

audit_questions_with_answers.question: Q5: More human or more robotic?
audit_questions_with_answers.answer: NEUTRAL — these are infrastructure decisions; no customer surface affected. The protocols (claim-before-doing, announce-before-doing) make agent handoff more careful, which is closer to 'more human' in the Wholesome Enrichment sense (taking care).

audit_questions_with_answers.question: Q6: Introduce risky autonomy?
audit_questions_with_answers.answer: NO — atoms have single-writer protocol; deprecation requires snapshot + entry both; unified Box dispatcher has fallback to legacy path so no live agents broke.

audit_questions_with_answers.question: Q7: Approval gate?
audit_questions_with_answers.answer: YES — atom completion requires concrete verification field; deprecation requires snapshot reference; steward writes default audit_only mode (operator confirms before local_write).

audit_questions_with_answers.question: Q8: Updates the right ledgers?
audit_questions_with_answers.answer: YES — DEC-002/003/004 each updated DECISIONS_LEDGER + INDEX + activity.jsonl. Atoms produced ATOMS.md/.json + receipts. Each steward promotion updated INDEX rows.

audit_questions_with_answers.question: Q9: Would a future agent understand why?
audit_questions_with_answers.answer: YES — every decision carries Why/Alternatives/Consequences/Do-Not-Undo blocks. Atom-Ledger §1 explicitly explains the freeze response that motivated atomization.

audit_questions_with_answers.question: Q10: Can this be rebuilt or audited later?
audit_questions_with_answers.answer: YES — activity.jsonl is append-only; ATOMS.json is canonical; receipts persist; sitemap + page ledgers track UI; git history captures all file moves.

findings_by_classification.summary: All 10 audit questions answered satisfactorily for all three architectural decisions. No alignment flags raised.

alignment_audit.recommended_section_11_alignment_note: 2026-04-30 Phase B alignment audit (synthesized via north_star_steward smoke test, ATOM-2026-04-30-0042). Today's work strongly aligned across NS-01, NS-03, NS-05, NS-07, NS-09, NS-10. One C-classification finding: unified-Box scaffolding inconsistency across 5 stewards (3 lighter / 2 heavier). Recommend follow-up atoms to bring 3 lighter Boxes (open_problems, north_star, plus the already-fixed-by-parallel-agent temporal_continuity) to the 6-file pattern. Phase B → Phase C tradeoff is explicit and on track per DEC-2026-04-29-002.

recommended_decisions_ledger_entries.title: Canonical unified-Box file pattern = 6 files (box.json + BOX.md + steward/3 + receipts/README.md)
recommended_decisions_ledger_entries.rationale: Parallel agent's 6-file pattern (used in file_directory + global_ledger Boxes) is richer and Box-Bus-ready; current 3-file lighter pattern (in open_problems + north_star) leaves Boxes under-equipped for Phase C runtime subscription. Promotion of this finding from C → B requires the operator's confirmation.

tradeoff_note.tradeoff_description: Phase B (steward fleet) completion velocity favored over depth of unified-Box scaffolding when authoring open_problems_steward (ATOM-0032) and north_star_steward (ATOM-0040). Strict acceptance criteria were met — agents.md + prompt.md mirroring global_ledger_steward — but the parent-Box layer (box.json + BOX.md + receipts/README.md) was authored only by the parallel agent's atoms (0036, 0044), not by Cowork.
tradeoff_note.ns_goals_weighed: NS-09 (handoff continuity — get stewards runnable fast) over NS-03 (Box-Based Local Intelligence — full Box scaffolding).
tradeoff_note.section_9_rule_applied: Velocity tradeoff acceptable when cleanup is explicit and recoverable. Recovery atoms can backfill the 3 lighter Boxes without breaking anything.
tradeoff_note.section_11_alignment_notes_entry_skeleto: Phase B steward velocity surfaced unified-Box scaffolding inconsistency. Recommend follow-up atoms to bring open_problems and north_star Boxes to 6-file pattern, OR accept the lighter shape with a Decisions Ledger entry. Currently classification C (unconscious tradeoff); promotion to B requires explicit operator confirmation.
tradeoff_note.review_trigger: When Phase C Box Bus runtime starts (ATOM under PROB-2026-04-30-006), the under-scaffolded Boxes will need box.json before they can subscribe. Backfill before that point, or accept that those 3 Boxes won't participate in early Phase C.

audit_question_surface.summary: All three architectural decisions answer the §10 audit questions satisfactorily — see classification F findings above.

outputs.wholesome_enrichment_caution: N/A for today's work. No client-facing copy or automation touched. The 4-test §6 protocol does not apply to infrastructure decisions. Recording explicitly so the steward's first audit doesn't conflate 'no violation' with 'no surface' — when customer surfaces are next touched, this steward should rerun the protocol.

No anomaly is flagged that isn't traceable to a quoted source.
